templates.name: How recent does the prescription need to be? Should it have an expiration date and what if it's expired?
templates.content: It needs to be an up-to-date prescription. If there is no expiration date noted, we can accept it. If it's noted as expired, you will need to get a new one.

templates.name: How soon can I expect my prescription glasses?
templates.content: Prescription orders can take up to 3 weeks to process before shipping. Once shipped, you'll get an email with a tracking number.

templates.name: Do you take insurance?
templates.content:
We do not currently accept vision insurance, however, we are happy to let you know that we can provide an itemized receipt that can be submitted to your insurance provider for reimbursement upon request.
We do accept FSA and HSA debit cards with a Visa or Mastercard logo on them. If you don't have one of these debit cards you can get an invoice from our support team to submit to your provider along with any other required documentation. All plans are different so you will need to refer to the guidelines of what is FSA/HSA eligible for your plan.

templates.name: Can I return prescription glasses?
templates.content: Prescription products are eligible for returns up to 14 days from receiving the product. You can check out our detailed return/exchange policy here: https://diff.attn.tv/a87q2aAvbbzM and initiate your return here: https://diff.attn.tv/aOk3Wuvq-dLm

templates.name: Do you have prescription sunglasses?
templates.content: Any of our prescription eyeglasses frames can be fitted with blue light technology or transformed into polarized sunglasses or photochromic glasses. You can check out our prescription eyeglasses collection here: https://www.diffeyewear.com/collections/prescription-eyeglasses

templates.name: Code doesn't work
templates.content: Sorry to hear that, I just tested the code and it worked on my end. Please also keep in mind that coupon codes are not valid on accessories, skin care, protection plans, select collaborations, charity items (including donations), gift cards, Iconica, Bella V, Iris, Zoe, prior purchases, taxes, or shipping.

templates.name: What does Progressive Lens mean?
templates.content:
Progressive lenses aid in an eye condition called Presbyopia. This is a very common vision condition that usually starts around the age of 40 and results in the loss of the ability to focus on near things. If you have an add power listed on your prescription you are presbyopic. As this condition progresses with age people usually transition from wearing single-vision lenses to multi-focal or progressive lenses.

You can read more about it here: https://diff.attn.tv/sl/DH7zwk4W

templates.name: I processed a return/exchange but I haven't received the return shipping label yet
templates.content: All returns/exchanges are manually approved by our CS team. If you processed it outside of office hours or during the weekend, we will review it on the next business day.

templates.name: Price adjustment
templates.content: We offer price adjustments for orders placed within the past 5 days.

templates.name: What is the difference between polarized and photochromic?
templates.content: Polarized lenses are specially designed to filter out harmful glare caused by reflections from flat surfaces like water or snow. This enables them to enhance visual clarity and reduce eye strain, making them ideal for outdoor activities. On the other hand, photochromic lenses are equipped with technology that allows them to darken in response to the intensity of UV light. This adaptive feature makes them convenient for individuals transitioning between different lighting conditions, as they automatically adjust to provide optimal visibility. Both polarized and photochromic lenses offer significant advantages, but their mechanisms make them better suited for different situations.

templates.name: Do you offer bifocal prescription sunglasses?
templates.content: I'm afraid we do not offer bifocals.
